contact.title: Contact support
contact.subtitle: We typically respond within one business day.
contact.messageMinLength: Message must be at least 20 characters.
contact.toast: Ticket #{{ticketId}} received. We'll reply by email.

hub.title: How can we help?
hub.description: Contextual guides for the page you're on — no search required.

metrics-deliverability.title: Understanding deliverability rate
metrics-deliverability.summary: What the headline percentage means for your workspace.
paragraphs: Deliverability rate shows the share of sent emails that reached recipients without a hard bounce during the selected period.
Use the period filter to compare trends over 15, 30, or 90 days. Domain filter narrows metrics to a single sending domain.
A sudden drop often correlates with list quality or DNS authentication — check bounce breakdown before changing volume.

metrics-bounce.title: Bounce rate and risk threshold
metrics-bounce.summary: Transient vs permanent bounces and the risk line.
paragraphs: Bounce rate combines transient (temporary mailbox issues), permanent (invalid addresses), and undetermined bounces.
The dashed risk line marks the threshold where sending reputation may be affected. Stay below it for healthy delivery.
Review the breakdown legend to see which bounce type drives your rate.

api-keys-create.title: Creating an API key
api-keys-create.summary: Generate a key with the right permission scope.
paragraphs: Open API keys and choose Create API key. Pick a descriptive name your team will recognize in audit logs.
Select the minimum permission needed — sending-only keys cannot manage billing or members.
Copy the secret immediately after creation. For security, it is shown only once.

api-keys-revoke.title: Revoking a compromised key
paragraphs: Create a replacement key first, update your services, then revoke the old key from the row actions menu.
Revoked keys stop working instantly. Active integrations using them will receive 401 responses.
Use search and permission filters to audit keys across large teams.

integrations-connect.title: Connecting an integration
integrations-connect.summary: Authorize third-party tools for your workspace.
paragraphs: Browse the catalog and choose Connect on the integration you need. Authorize access in the dialog to complete the mock OAuth flow.
Connected integrations show a green status indicator with a Disconnect option.

integrations-disconnect.title: Disconnecting safely
integrations-disconnect.summary: Remove access without affecting other workspaces.
paragraphs: Hover a connected card and open the actions menu to disconnect. Confirm in the dialog to revoke access immediately.
Disconnect stops new events from flowing to the integration. Reconnect anytime from the same card.
If a production integration fails, disconnect and reconnect to refresh credentials in your real deployment.

members-invite.title: Inviting teammates
paragraphs: Choose Invite member, enter an email, and select a role. Invited users appear with an Invited badge until they accept.
Owners can manage billing and delete the workspace. Admins manage settings and members. Members have limited access.
Use role filter and search to find people quickly in larger workspaces.

members-seats.title: Seat limits on your plan
members-seats.summary: How active and invited members count toward seats.
paragraphs: Both active and invited members count toward your seat limit on paid plans.
The toolbar shows seats used versus your plan allowance. Upgrade billing when you need more seats.
Removing a member frees a seat immediately after confirmation.

billing-plans.title: Plans and upgrades
paragraphs: Billing overview lists available plans with feature comparisons. Upgrade opens a confirmation dialog with proration notes.
Trial workspaces show remaining trial days on the overview banner.
Payment method must be on file before upgrading from trial.

billing-invoices.title: Invoices and receipts
billing-invoices.summary: Find past charges and download PDFs.
paragraphs: The Past Invoices section lists date, amount, invoice number, and status for each billing period.
Paid invoices include a download action for your records.
Billing email on the overview receives invoice notifications.

usage-summary.title: Reading usage meters
usage-summary.summary: How plan limits and consumption are displayed.
paragraphs: Each row shows consumed amount versus your plan limit for the current billing cycle.
Circular indicators fill as you approach limits. Seats and email volume are common early constraints.
Usage may take up to an hour to refresh after changes in the workspace.

usage-upgrade.title: Unavailable features on your plan
usage-upgrade.summary: When metrics show Upgrade instead of a meter.
paragraphs: Some capabilities are only included on higher tiers — Monthly Active SSO Users and Storage Image Transformations appear as unavailable on Free and Pro.
Click Upgrade to open the plan picker and compare tiers without leaving settings.
After upgrading, locked rows become active meters on your next usage refresh.

onboarding-activation.title: Completing workspace activation
onboarding-activation.summary: Finish the checklist to unlock the full workspace.
paragraphs: Activation steps guide you through domain setup, API keys, and your first send — tailored to this demo product.
You can open settings pages while activation is pending; the workspace home redirects here until complete.
Check off each step as you finish. Progress saves automatically in this demo environment.

account-profile.title: Updating your profile
account-profile.summary: Change display name and account details.
paragraphs: Profile settings apply to you across all workspaces — not a single organization.
Display name appears in the user menu and member lists where your email is shown.

account-sessions.title: Managing active sessions
account-sessions.summary: Review devices and sign out remotely.
paragraphs: Sessions lists browsers and locations for recent sign-ins. Your current session is marked.
Revoke individual sessions or sign out all other devices from the Security page.

general-getting-started.title: Getting started with your workspace
general-getting-started.summary: Create a workspace and invite your team.
paragraphs: After sign-up you create a workspace name and slug. Slugs appear in URLs and API references.
Invite teammates from Members settings. Each workspace has its own billing, API keys, and metrics.

general-security.title: Security best practices
general-security.summary: Protect API keys and member access.
paragraphs: Never commit API keys to source control. Use environment variables on the server.
Review member roles regularly and remove inactive accounts.
Enable two-factor authentication when available in your production deployment.

general-support.title: When to contact support
general-support.summary: Issues we can help with fastest.
paragraphs: Include your workspace slug, approximate time of the issue, and any error messages.
For delivery problems, mention the domain and a sample message ID if available.
Use the impact selector so we can prioritize urgent production outages.
